long_description:
Alerted by slack.

Volume is locked by a snapshot, which is stuck in RSNAP_ACCEPT state.

snapshot id : r018-7038e59f-b365-4632-8bd8-ef4737978cda
namespace: 0f628e88c6594675bbefa097a63b9293
volume: r018-b20f9db0-1c44-47c0-83d6-877392873caa
Incident source: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C02JSL04PU0/p1765011731206169 - HUANG XING
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: Snapshot stuck in RSNAP_ACCEPT LON
SME Findings: sme-team: SME Findings
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps:
Signature of Issue:
Jira/Github/Other Tickets:
Category/Subcategory:
Timeline Summary:

- send info as requested.
- SRE will raise a new dev request to check snapshot status.
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed / Not Available / URL
Caused by Change/Incident:
Warroom:
Meeting URL:
Outage Start:
Outage End:
Detection Time:

Mitigation Time:
CIE pilot/co-pilot/squad names:
CORE Manager names:

Team mitigated the CIE:
Next Update Time:
current_status_next_steps:
Debugged with SRE.

1) Run this command from a RIAS jumppod:

kubectl label storagesnapshot -n <namespace> <snapshot-id> foo=bar

2) Check the snapshot again (wait a little)

kubectl get storagesnapshot -n <namespace> <snapshot id> -o yaml

3) Check the volume spec:

kubectl get volumespec -A -l ExternalVolumeID=<volume id> -o yaml

 -- runbook link --
